text: Ghastly Gala
33 ABY
Zubindi Hakoon
Dreven

Amidala (NPC), Atsvara Tarasar (NPC), Cricket (NPC), Darth Malign, Janus Tarasar (NPC), "Jessalyn" (NPC), Lynae Cassius, May (NPC), Myst (NPC), Nara Rhen, Quaye Oswent (NPC), Sentinel (NPC), Ssanyu Natrulon, Tray Garos (NPC), V1C3 (NPC), Vanilla (NPC), Veruca Violet (NPC), Xa Fel Waiters (NPC), Zubindi Hakoon
Silverwood House of Fashion, Tahn Consortium, Galactic Imperium, Sith

The aesthetic of the infamous Basca Starker, the man responsible for the pandemic that wiped out most of Caspar's population five years ago is 'celebrated' at a party to be held at Castle Tsabak, the former Imperial palace on Dreven.
Imperial Hall of Heroes <G01> - Castle Tsabak: Dreven

A marvel of architecture, this vast, impressive chamber does not appear as though it should be possible by accepted laws of design. The Hall is a rectangle which stretches from the gilded doors at one end to the speaker's platform at the other. At the centre of the room there is stone altar surrounded by a garden. Laying atop the altar there appears to be a body.

Two makeshift stages have been set up in the room on either side of the altar, each equidistant from the altar and the outer wall. They are both circular in shape and constructed of wansook pine. The one north of the altar has been painted black and is cluttered with a variety of instruments and amplifiers, the rage-metal band Dooku's Droogs are set up here. The one south of the altar has been painted white and has a far more modest set up with only microphones and speakers, the Theelin Diva group, the Five Elements are set up here.

The vaulted ceiling looms several dozen meters over the black marble floor with no apparent means of support, and it's curve suggests it was designed for acoustics. Adding to the sense of improbability, a plethora of vines dangle in the air above without quite seeming to visibly connect to anything. A layer of black smoke permeates the room at floor level though not so dense that the floor itself can not be glimpsed. The audience chamber is visible above, and the entire chamber is evenly lit, though the light is dim and indirect.

Around the hall of the room, a number of white larmalstone busts have been placed on pedestals. Each one is of a famous bioterrorist. A single pedestal is covered with a crimson cloth - hiding the bust underneath before it is unveiled. Three makeshift bars have been set up at convenient locations, also around the edges of the room. Waiters can be found constantly going back and forth from the bars to restock on drinks to bring around the hall.

In addition to the drinks an extensive menu of food is paraded around the hall for the dining pleasure of the guests. Keeping with the party's theme the food is divided between black platter offerings which while appealing to some cultures are considered gross by general galactic standards and white platter offerings which are generally considered more palatable by the mainstream.

%tIt had been many years since the Hall of Heroes had hosted any gathering of consequence. In fact it had been some time since Castle Tsabak was a place of importance. In recent years Dreven was on the edge of space that both the Imperium and Republic seemed to want but once they had it did not give much care about it. But an Imperial Palace was in Zubindi's mind the perfect place to fete the aesthetic of a man who was Emperor Vadim's instrument of destruction.%r%tThe Hall of Heroes is absolutely teaming with people who have come to the party either as guests or to work it, with the line between the two not always easy to tell. Though there is no exact dress code for the party, one way the expression of theme of Grotesques and Odalisques has manifested is in the colour palette with most costumes and outfits theme around black or white.%r%tIn a stark contrast from the days of the old Empire, the Hall of Heroes is just as filled with 'aliens' as it is with humans and near-humans. Devaronians, Givins, Kubaz, Neimoidians, Nikto, and Xa Fel are particularly well represented at the party among the very staff members bringing around food and drink amongst other duties.%r%tAmbling about near the speaker's platform, in a class all his own is the proprietor of the Silverwood House of Fashion, Zubindi Hakoon. Rejecting the black and white theme he has others modelling his own over the top outfit is a mix of green, blue, and purple. The line between fashion designer and pimp is unclear in the outfit and indeed in the party itself.%r%tAs the party is getting underway in earnest the two bands performing it engage in final sounds checks. The harsh and grating tones of the rage-metal band Dooku's Droogs are heavily contrasted by the dulcet tones of the Five Elements, the Theelin singing group operating on the other stage.

%t"Slow down, my titty-cups are slipping!" The melodious binary hoots of one Cricket McKenzie, R2 astromech extraordinaire, gradually make themselves heard above the ambient chatter of arriving guests and last-minute preparations. Said astromech has festooned himself with red plastic drinking cups on a string that project across the front of his squat cylindrical torso, and a leopard-print paint job -- meant to approximate a man's tight-fitting briefs -- decorates his undercarriage. "Damnit, Miss Atsvara!" he shrieks, pausing in front of one of the stages near Zubindi, "Help me!"

%tAtsvara turns around to look at Cricket upon hearing her name called and giggles, "So that is why we ran out of cups back at the warehouse. I can't wait to see what your girlfriend thinks when she sees a picture of this." The golden-skinned girl for her own part has taken her inspiration from the grotesques, her body covered in tattoos and warpaint.

%t"I cannot help being fabulous," Cricket replies, wheeling up beside his young companion, "Sacrifices had to be made in the name of fashion; I'm sure Zubindi would agree. But you look very good yourself, Miss Atsvara. I like the tattoos, do they mean anything?" He himself would love to have tattoos, but his cow of a so-called master had once made a comment, and ever since then, he has had a complex about it. Stupid fat Johanna and her stupid fat opinions! Nobody asked her, anyway! Why can't he cover himself in symbols earned by ritually disembowling his enemies?! "Oh! And... I brought the Sock-Checker," he adds conspiratorially.

%tThere's business casual, office casual, military casual, and then there's Lynae casual. Which translates to polish but not prissy. Dressed not, specifically, to kill. But as the situation arises, it's important to be prepared. And since this is o small event, wardrobe choices are no small matter. Which is how Lynae is wearing what she's wearing and if the host, hostess, hostex, hostetrix? has issues, they can bring it up with her, face to face! There had better be an open bar.

%t"Mean anything? Not most of them. At least I don't think so. That wasn't really the point. Of course naturally the number 7 does." Atsvara smirks, "It isn't all it is cracked up to be though. I am not /entirely/ sure how long it is going to take these tattoos to fade." She looks around the room searching for familiar faces among the guests. She then looks back at Cricket, "Sock-Checker? I am definitely going to need a drink." Flagging down the first Xa Fel she sees, "I need a Blue Milkshake, stat."

%tThere isn't quite a change within the 'usual' attire for the Falleen, rather tending to dress for occasions that she could slip into a party with. This being no different situation. The heels come to grope across the ground echoing out a sound with each pass that simply gets lost within the natural noise pollution of the area. The features of her face are like a mask has been settled across them, not a single physical presentation of emotion resting along them and even the flesh-tone speaks of a 'neutral' setting for Ssanyu. The amethyst orbs search out across the sea of faces as if searching for someone or something in particular.

%tAs everyone and everything falls into place Zubindi walks onto the speaker's platform. "On behalf of the Silverwood House of Fashion and the Tahn Consortium I want to welcome you here to the Hall of Heroes where we are reflecting on the artistic image of Basca Starker." He makes a gesture out towards the middle of the room at which point a giant spot is turned on facing towards an altar which appears to contain the corpse of Basca.%r%t"Among many accomplishments he developed the cure for the Sariphage pandemic which wipe out nearly three billion people on Caspar....of course he was also the c*nt who develop the virus that killed them all in the first place. He was the seemingly dashing young doctor with an eye to bringing beauty into the world all the while corrupted by an Emperor who thought nothing out of wiping out a planet. He was also a beast, literally infested with a Sith Spawn, which somehow was the part of him that actually seemed to have an iota of conscience."%r%tZubindi pauses for a few moments to allow the full perverse concept to be absorbed the his audience. "So we present to you Grotesques and Odalisques, a requiem of Basca Starker. Tonight we examine the duality of man, the duality of art, the duality of taste. We bring to you the beauty and the beast all as one. Modelled for your viewing pleasure. Brought to your ears by Dooku's Droogs and the Five Elements. Deposited on your taste buds from a lavish menu of food and drink. He motions again towards the altar in the centre, "And delivered to your soul by an echo of the man himself and the woman he loved.

%t"Fascinating," that's the only word that Lynae can come up with. What a roiling boil of rage Zubindi's words manage to evoke. Rage and fascination but mostly rage. Maybe a trifle smidgen of professional curiosity. But mostly.. yeah, she can work with rage. Rage is warm and fuzzy, like a cloak made out of Horansi pelt. Lynae snags a drink off of the tray of one of those ubiquitous servers who appear at these sorts of events, possibly mulling over the notion of using the fumes from the alcohol along with a spark to set the corpse of Basca on fire.

%t"What the /fuck/," Cricket bleats, none too thrilled by the presence of the cloned corpse, "I thought they turned his ass into fertilizer?!" Alas, 'they' had not. He approaches the altar, his makeshift bikini top askew, left cup hoisted higher than the right as the plastic bobbles slightly with movement. Sadly, Astvara has not yet deigned to lend an opposable-thumbed hand in assistance of the matter.

%tAtsvara looks on surprised at Cricket's reaction, "I thought he was the one whose's 'sock' you want to measure? Of course he is here. How can we properly mourn his feckless....fuck I can't think of a good enough adjective. I could have sworn I sent you a memo." And by memo she means a larger trove of detailed confidential documents. Hey at least for once it is with her own clearance instead of a forgery.

%tA man in white approaches from behind Atsvara putting his hands on her shoulders and remarking to Cricket and Lynae, "The Ministry of Health kept the clone, feeling it might come handy at some point. Obviously this is not what they had in mind, but I think Zubindi was able to sew some strings."

%t"Never turn the dead into fertilizer until you're certain their corpse doesn't contain any unforeseen contaminates. This one," and Lynae eyes the corpse again before turning a wry ghost of a smile at Cricket and Atsvara, "is more likely toxic than can be managed with fire. Has anyone considered just tossing the remains into the gravity pull of the nearest star and testing what happens?" This said while she casts a sidelong glance at Cricket's 'attire', her head finally tilting, "Is this deliberate?" she gestures with the drink she's holding to mis-aligned cups. "With fashion, I can't ever be certain."

%tA being that looks like the spitting image of Jedi Master Jessalyn Valios, minus being a decimetre or so shorter, stands stoically in front of the altar in the middle of the room. She is soon approached by three women coming to her from varying directions in the room: a Pantoran in a kimono, an albino wearing a pair of feathered wings and not much else, and a woman in a robe that looks like a former queen and senator from Naboo.%r%tAs the women gather together the Pantoran speaks, "I know I have said this before but I want to thank you all again for your help in this affair. We could not have pulled this off with you." The other women nod and smile to Veruca, with Amidala replying, "Hell I am just happy to get the fuck off Ngor Nghal."

%tMemos, who has time for those?! "Uh... I must have missed that memo," the astromech warbles before falling silent while the official gives his explanation as to why the accursed corpse wasn't fed to the hogs. Hrmph... he's not wrong, though. Keeping Basca's clone on ice -- or in stasis, as it were -- probably has its uses. "Miss Lynae!" he exclaims joyfully as the physician addresses him, "Yes, you're right, one never knows what kind of /goop/ Starker might have jammed himself full of. But still, launching him into the nearest supernova would be satisfying. Say... do you mind straightening out my top? It is /not/ supposed to be crooked!"

%tOne of the Xa Fel waiters approaches Cricket, Astvara, and Lynae, his lumpen face failing an attempt at a pleasant smile. Extending a platter towards them, causing a lesion on his arm to swell nearing to eruption, he croaks out in a hoarse voice, "Micromite pate?"

%tZubindi gets off the speakers platform and starts to look around the room. He starts off towards the centre of the room catching Veruca Violet as she walks away from the other women, "Where the fuck is he? Like what the everloving fuck. As the waiter returns with Astvara's blue milkshake he looks to the man, "I think it is time for an Ithorian brandy or several. But lets start with just the one."%r%tThe appearance of the other waiter startles Zubindi for a moment for some reason before he takes some of the offered dish, "This is exactly what the doctor ordered. Okay probably not the doctor but certainly me."
%tAtsvara takes her blue milkshake clearfully from the first waiter but shakes her head at the second. "No thanks I am waiting for some of those Kullgroon Drops to make their way around."

%t"Variables," Lynae muses as she finishes the drink and angles slightly to set the drink down before she adjusts Crickets attire. "There's crooked and then there's misaligned and then there's artistic freedom," she makes another tiny adjustment until the cups are as prescisely aligned as possible. Without the use of a level, of course. Did she bring one? of course not. Probably not. She also casts another of those /sidelong/ glances toward Jessalyn, back around to Zubindi and Atsvara with a smile until the waiter approaches with a tray of ... "Now that looks unpleasant." No, she's NOT looking at the appetizers, but instead the swelling lesion. Her left hand rests on he medkit at her side, some instincts never die. Her eyes lift, again, this time flicking from face to facade and back again to the waiter. "Unless that's part of the entertainment," making a question of it without being obnoxiously pushy about it.

%tSomething between a grunt and a shriek escapes Cricket at the sight of the Xa Fel's pustule, a high-pitched "HNNNGHGH!!" briefly assaulting the ears of those present, newly-straightened cups jiggling as he wheels to hide behind Lynae (she's a doctor, she can take a face full of juicy pus! she's used to it!). His cowardice is probably unbecoming of a Biscuit Baron executive, but he cannot help it. Something unholy and smelly and... /crawly/, probably... is inside that creepy lesion!

%tThe waiter seems pleased when Zubindi takes some of the pate, though it may be difficult to tell. "I'll let them know," he wheezes towards Atsvara. Before he can depart, the skin loses its tension and the lesion splits in a slurry of ichor. He mumbles a homeworld curse, unsure of what to do.

%tAs a young Sarian woman carrying a white platter of nuts near Veruca, the Pantoran girl motions her over. Grabbing a handful of Warra nuts. "You would think given the family business I would be tired of these by now. At least I left my boyfriend at home, he would be like a fuzzling on haggleday."

%t"Don't worry, I'll manage this," Lynae's tone of voice is actually SOOTHING toward Cricket. Soothing. Reassuring. That's probably going to frighten someone. She's stepping forward just as the lesion splits in a slurry of viscous ichor, a truly noxious and unique smell spreading outward as further proof of the pudding. She snags the recently empty glass from where she'd set it, positions it just beneath the spreading mess and angles it to capture the sludge and prevent it from spreading to far. "Hold still for a moment," trusting the waiter to be reasonable and not flail about or faint, though she does fix a stern glare at the waiter - daring the waiter to faint and make more of a mess.

%tThe Xa Fel waiter seems stunned at the attention he's given. "It's fine. Happens all the time," he tries to explain. Judging by the collection of scars and cysts on his exposed body, there is probably some truth to it. He points his pockmarked, bulbous nose towards the glass in Lynae's hand. "Can I have that back. Unless you were planning on using it...?"

%tIf it was not for the faint lift and fall of the chest, the Falleen could easily double as a piece of decoration standing amongst the crowd. Finally, there's more movement than the simple breath that keeps her bodily functions executing as need be naturally so. Her chin draws the decoration of her head towards the side. The amethyst orb shift out once more towards those gathering,. Fingers of the left hand curve slightly inwards, fingers tap against the side of her leg momentarily before falling flat once more. Ssanyu exhales out a small breath through lightly flared nostrils before the heels take life once more upon the ground, drawing her to weave through the crowd towards a direction of a bar.

%tThe last time Cricket had been this traumatized, it had involved something... gaping. "AUAHGHAHGHGH," he opines shrilly as Lynae bravely catches the foul fluid in a glass, her soothing (!!) tone notwithstanding, "NOOOOO, don't TOUCH it!" Oh gods! She was going to get infected, and then HE was going to get infected, and then everything would be ruined!!

%t'Jessalyn' walks around the altar taking her pruning shears to each of rose starting from the left and working around until she reaches the other side. As each rose is 'cut' its white flower is replaced with black flower spewing mould. The flowers, however, are actually holographic and the act of being 'cut' merely cues the change from one appearance to the other. As she reaches the end of her route she kneels in front of the altar and the body, "Woe to us. His beauty is gone from this world. His charm is gone from our life."

%tThe skill to arch only ONE eyebrow upward is well worth the time invested to make the facial expression upon command. And Lynae does exactly that. Arch just ONE eyebrow before she surrenders the glass to the waiter, careful to avoid touching any of it's contents. "Is it safe to assume that the contents of the lesion will not be included in any of the food or drink?" asked in a tone of voice that conveys only mild curiosity. The mild curiosity turns, a moment later, into a slightly baffled and equally/slightly horrified expression at the words from 'Jessalyn'. A wary look is aimed at the glass, "What was in my drink?"

%t"Uhm...," is the uncertain reply from the waiter. Perhaps misunderstanding the question he continues, "Well, if you want some more, we can go back to the kitchen?" The hope in his tone is unmistakenable as he glances in the direction of the scullery.

%t"BITCH WHAT IS WRONG WITH YOU," Cricket demands of the waiter from where he stands behind Lynae, his pimped-out designer electrum taser now crackling to life, "Nobody wants your disgusting fluids! Keep your juices to yourself!

%tAtsvara puts a hand on Cricket's dome, "Calm the fuck down. We are celebrating Sith Spawn mass murder. The theme is literally grotesques. He was asked to be here. Remember we are dignified business executives." She them removes her hand to adjust her beads and feathers." She steps away for a moment motion back to Cricket to no one in particular, "I love this guy."

%t"No, that's quite alright," and Lynae shares a glance with Atsvara who, also, is trying to keep Cricket from using the electrum taser in more than a wand-waving maneuver. "I should have examined the food and drink menu with more care before deciding to attend. Feel free to return to your duties." She also, oh so casually, takes a step to impede Cricket's immediate path to advance on the waiter. Death should always be deliberate!

%tWhat first starts as a contortion of anger on the Xa Fel's face from the droid's admonishment, quickly turns to a welling tear of rejection. "Thank you...," he mumbles to Atsvara and quickly turns in embarrassment to retreat to the safety of the kitchens. Unfortunately, the platter of pate tumbles to the ground, the thick tan appetizer splattering and mixing with discharge from another lesion. Another homeworld curse bursts forth from the waiter's lips.

%tFrom the black stage the Herglic at the Nalargon stand up at his microphone and excitedly says, "Wow this audience is sick! I am Count Whaleface and we are Dooku's Droogs." His fellow band members gesticulate wildly. "We have been busy touring in support of our new album 'The Tarkin Corridor,' but we couldn't turn down the chance to join all you freaks tonight." He looks to his other band members than back out at this audience, "This one isn't ours, but I am sure it will enrage just this same." With that he sits back down at his instrument and the band proceeds to play a metal rendition of the Imperial March.

%t"Hello again, everyone, and welcome to what may be the gala to end all galas," Nara says cheerfully into her camdroid's 'face', smiling widely to show her apparently heartfelt good cheer and enthusiasm. "The theme was labeled, 'Grotesques and Odalisques,' though I must admit I don't quite know what that means... but I'm very much inclined to seek out a fellow guest and see what I can find out! At the very least, I can get a drink and chat up some potential new friends, and who doesn't love to socialize?" Accompanied by a wider smile.

%tThe young woman is dressed in black, to the tune of a wraparound minidress and stiletto-heeled knee boots of shiny black nerf leather. Gold earrings and locket and a matching belt made of a chain of gold links that rings softly as she moves breaks up the darkness of her wardrobe, and a pair of gold mini-bracers seem to carry a wristchrono and the backup controls to her camdroid. A black choker with a stylized gold clasp bears a concealed mic to catch her spoken words, but a feedback-shielded hand mic in one hand serves as a prop, and to catch the words of interviewees.

%tMalign didn't remember the last time he was on Dreven, likely it involved carbonite and the usurping of a failing Emperor. The world had lost its importance onward, Selene became the central aspect of the Empire under his tenure and was again that under the Imperium. Who even controlled the system? He mused, ultimately, he didn't care. The party was in full swing, the festivities could be sensed outside of Castle Tsabak as he and his menagerie of minions flowed behind him. A party that of the grotesque and obscene, sounds fun, but what is grotesque to a Sith Alchemist?%r%tMalign enters the party in a simple set of robes, the cloak earthen resembling a Republic-era Jedi. Behind him, Quaye, dressed similarly follows him, a long braid of hair resting over her left shoulder. The Dark Lord glances over the party, "Trust your feelings, and stay in the present my Padawan." He shrugs with a half-smile.%r%tBehind the pair, a more comic site, a small squadron of mutant Wookiees but old Clone-Trooper armor pulled out of the heap of history on some requisition world was awkwardly modified and worn loosely under across their massive forms, their blackened quills and razor sharp claws on full display but comically muted by the white sections armour carelessly hoisted upon them.%r%tThe Dark Lord and Quaye stop a few meters in to observe the crowd finding a corner to 'role-play' their own concept of disgusting.

%tThere's a pause within the steps that are bringing her to her current destination as the louder voices come to rise above the rest of the crowd. The Falleen's gaze moves over towards the direction of the gathering without a single blink to cross over the gaze, and then she's shifting towards the direction of the band at the announcement. Still, there's no emotions to surface upon Ssanyu's face but there's a few prickling of a red hue that decorates certain areas of the green flesh not covered by the fabric of the dress. Another exhale, and then she turns to fasten her pace to arrive at one of the bars.

%tThe arrival of the Imperator himself is noticed by some of the attendings. One in particular the white suited man near Atsvara, steps away and moves towards Malign's direction. Janus nods towards Malign before moving over to one of the bars, not actually going over to speak with him.

%t"I -- I'm SORRY," the astromech sighs loudly at Atsvara's admonition. It just isn't fair: nobody had warned him about pustule-ridden abominations offering guests the chance to lick/suck their boils and boil-contents. There's a reason he generally parts company with Johanna when she visits her deranged brother, mutants are simply NOT his thing, much less /leaking/ ones! Caspian space is much more civilized! And speaking of Johanna's brother... the taser crackles once more, Cricket's dislike of Malign not limited to his weird menagerie.

%tLynae eyes the waiter as he departs, the sound/scent of another lesion erupting & mixing with the pate appetizers makes her shudder ever so faintly. "Really, really should have read that menu better," the words quietly exhaled in a low voice as the front man for the band addresses the crowd before a metal rendition of the Imperial March bounces off of the walls. And then Malign and his entourage arrive and she's distracted by Cricket and the taser Cricket is wielding. "Alright, Cricket, lets not do anything ... hasty."

%tA couple of the models working the party stop near the rage-metal band. Vanilla motions towards the band and whispers to Amidala, "I hope they play Order 69, I love that song." "You mean Order 66 don't you?" asks Amidala in reply. "Oh right," Vanilla replies as her enthusiasm audibly fades.

%tA MSE droid quickly wheels up, assisting the waiter with cleanup duties, though some of the mixture coats its wheels. Without saying another word, the embarrassed and flustered Xa Fel waiter retreats off the main floor, hoping he is not dismissed from his duties for the rest of the evening. Tracks of micromite pate lead back to the scullery, perhaps to be cleaned up by another maintenance droid later.

%tThe Jedi-dressed Sith grabs at Quaye's cuff, "Come Padawan, your Master needs a drink." The Sith smirks, "Sentinel, watch out for uhh, Confederacy battle droids or something..." Janus' nod doesn't go unnoticed, Malign knows how to tilt his head too and does so, "Remember Quaye, this is a mission for peace and the Republic or something." He murmurs moving toward a bar and waving down someone, "Ithorian Brandy, Bottle, two glasses, need it for meditation support." He turns to Quaye, "Brandy? Mist? Last time I did one of those and a few other things it was two X-wings versus the New Republic fleet."%r%tFor their part the mutant guard keep their distance, to allow party goers to approach and converse with the Imperator, if they wish, but they were just starting to find their party legs, "Brandy then." He finalises the order realising the server was likely waiting, "We will be on our best behaviour representing the Jedi Order." His eyes shift toward Cricket briefly recognising the droid, his hand instinctually sliding toward the lightsaber on his belt for a brief second before their bottle and classes arrive, "Alright, Quaye, use those Jedi instincts and find us a table.

%t"Pardon me, dear sentient, but may I join you?" Nara asks the Falleen (Ssanyu) at the bar politely, her smile warming. "I'm Nara Rhen, and I'm recording for my fashion and culture column. Tell me, are you enjoying yourself? And do you understand the theme? I confess I do not, so I would be deeply grateful for any help in that regard."%r%tShe looks back over her shoulder as Malign and his entourage enter. "Ladies and gentlebeings, I believe that's the Imperator and his wife... or is it girlfriend? I'm not quite sure. They've certainly got a strange entourage. I can't quite tell whether they're Ewoks or Wookiees... though I always had trouble telling the difference anyway, honestly." Nara may be pretty and sweet, but nobody who's viewed her blog ever described her as smart.

%t'Jessalyn' begins to walk around the altar reversing her direction from before. As she ones again 'cuts' each rose the black mould spewing hologram is replaced by a white rose once again. When she finally reaches back to the front of the altar she kneels in front of the body again, "In the wake of his mass murder, a new Caspar arises like a phoenix from the ashes. Stronger, more united, more dedicated, more dangerous."

%tWhile certain high-profile individuals would gain particular looks their direction, the Falleen's attention over towards the too familiar face of Malign is simply another one of those reptilian stares. The non-blinking stare lasts for a touch longer than one would deem 'comfortable' but it's quickly drawn aside by a voice directed towards her direction. A glance is made towards the empty stools that she has not yet resided into one before turning back towards Nara, watching without a smile to grace her own lips. At the mention of Malign's entourage, it brings another glance towards the direction. "You could ask for identification of their species for clarification of educational purposes." As with her face, the tone is flat - emotionless. "You may join me if you wish. There is no restrictions in attending the space near my presence." Ssanyu's eyes once more moves out towards the general gathering. "In regards to theme, I would assume that is for debate, as it seems to be dependent on whom you ask. My own enjoyment is not something that needs to be of note."

%tArriving from some unnoticed corner of the room beauty and beast come in one package as another white clad model comes in with a vornskr following in front. The vornskr is unique, seeming to have a shadow around her as if not fully in phase with our dimension. Following the insticts of the beast, they both head slowly towards one of the bars.

%tHasty? What nonsense is Lynae speaking? Cricket would /never/ do anything hasty! His taser falls silent and retreats back into its slot, a pincer-arm clasping a measuring device emerging in its stead. "Fine," he grumps, "I will use my Sock-Checker now. I hope yours are up to snuff, Doctor Cassius!" Without explaining further, he wheels off towards Malign and Quaye in their heretical Jedi robes, his dander rising at the Imperator's rudeness. "Stand still," the astromech commands the human, "Submit to a sock check." He presses the device, a holographic display of "LAME" appearing after a moment of scanning Malign's footwear. "Stand back so you don't get infected," he says to a passing topless beauty, "The Imperator is LAME."

%t"My socks are just fine," Lynae's remark is aimed mostly at the back of Cricket's dome as she follows along in his wake. She even snags another drink, somewhere along the way, and is being not-at-all-casual about using a tool from her medkit to check the contents OF the drink before sampling in. This as Cricket's announcement of LAME status catches her by surprise and it's enough to make her laugh. Not a quiet, tame, polite laugh. But a surprised burst of pure delighted amusement. "You needed a tool to determine that?"

%tIn response to the most recent of the 'Jessalyn' musings Atsvara remarks, "With the way things have changed since the pandemic you could say that the modern Caspar has been birthed from Basca's vagina." Veruca nearly spits out her wine at her friend's remark, "You could, but why in the ever loving fuck would you want to?" Atsvara giggles, "Shock value."

%t"I'm not sure the Imperator would deign to speak to a lowly holojournalist," Nara replies, just slightly nervously, to Ssanyu as she takes her seat at the bar. "Perhaps if I were from a major news holonetwork, maybe, but not a mere independent. Though it's not impossible that someone else knows what those odd guards are. I shall keep my eyes and ears open."%r%t"Anyway, you know my name. What should I call you?" she asks the Falleen. "If you'd rather not use your real name, I won't mind."

%tIt didn't take the pseudo-Jedi duo long to be almost halfway through their bottle as some of the party's attention was seeping their way, Cricket, being the most oppressive of it. A sock inspection, the Sith Lord raised his robe to reveal no socks, "I guess that does make it lame." As the droid rolls away with his sign, "And I was going to make that droid governor of Coruscant today, I guess that doesn't count anymore. Korolov can keep it I guess." He shrugs as Quaye pours him another glass.%r%tThe Sith's eyes turn toward a Jessalyn, "That looks familiar." He shrugs, "Did I make that one too?" he whispers toward Quaye, and she gives him a small smack on the head, "Stay in character Master Jedi." She turns into her own drink, leaving the Imperator to try to mentally do a roll check on his circle members and where they should be.%r%tThe Sith made a slight gesture toward Sentinel and his friends, and their red-eyes shifted toward the Jessalyn and the Wookiee shakes his head. The Dark Lord nods, "This brandy is doing the trick."

%tA twitch of a corner of her mouth indicates that something may begin to appear upon them but alas, nothing happens besides for them splitting in order to speak once more. "I am sure that the Imperator would be delighted in order to ensure that even the most lowly of holojournalists are indulged of the words that he could impart upon others that would be spread through the galaxy as.." A beat. "close to his true words as possible. As one in his position is often found himself within others twisting his own words so what better way than to impart his words upon someone that would want to ensure that even down to his nature of flavouring words would be written true to word?" Ssanyu shifts slightly within her weight, the amethyst orbs continuing to watch Nara equally as much as she's watching the action between the sock inspection and others lingering around. "Ssanyu Natrulon," she states for name.

%tLest anyone think that the only offerings are the party are gross, a Mirialan scantily clad in white comes around carrying a platter that is appropriately enough filled with Mirial Teacakes. As she passes by Veruca Violet, the Pantoran takes one of the teacakes in one hand while continuing to hold her wine glass in the other. She makes a shrugging gesture towards Zubindi in the distance. Someone was still missing.
%tCrisis averted? Or maybe delayed. Lynae salutes Tyler with the drink she's holding and moves along after Cricket, ready to continue the tally of LAME vs NOT LAME.

%t"I see... perhaps I shall, at that. Sadly, I really must go for now, as I've many guests to talk to. It was delightful to meet you, Ssanyu Natrulon, and I hope we cross paths again, whether tonight or another time. Thank you again for taking time out to chat with me," Nara replies warmly to Ssanyu, rising from her stool to take her leave. Her enthusiasm and warmth do seem quite genuine, though her fans may already know this from her prior podcasts.

%tThe intoxicant laden smoke wafts through the room lingering longer around a section of creeper vines. From behind a white bloom, a blaze bug falls from its perch into the crowd, landing near Nara. Waggling its abdomen and legs, it begins a mating dance, seemingly convinced that her holorecording equipment is a suitable mate.

%tAs the conversation around her seems to die out, Atsvara saunters off looking for other familiar faces. The visage of a familiar journalist catches her eyes and the young business mogul makes her way in the direction of Nara and her camera's new insect suitor.

%tNara takes a startled step back, shrieking in surprise and fear as the insect falls to the deck! She'd hoped it would scurry off somewhere, but it seems like it's not going anywhere... if anything, it's enthusiastically staying put!%r%t"Um, can someone help me, please? There's a... a rather large bug here, and I'm not sure what it's doing!" she calls timidly to those around her. Spotting Atsvara, she raises a hand to bar the girl from going near the insect. "Better stay back," she cautions her gently. "It might be dangerous."

%tWith a platter of Kullgroon Drops in hand, another Xa Fel waiter, with a hunched back and a noticeably shorter leg, hobbles towards Atsvara and Nara. "I was told that you wished some of these?" he asks in a velvety smooth voice. If not for his bulging eyes, patchwork of stringy hair, and other deformities, he would be considered quite the handsome fellow. The glowing bug, with its menacing mating dance, catches his attention, eliciting a gasp. "Oh dear!"

%tAtsvara quickly pluck up a couple of Kullgroon Drops as soon as the waiter is close enough, "Fank you," she mutters as a her face is already full of food. After taking a few moments to swallow the delicious product from the hulls of broken ships and damaged droids she looks back to Nara. "A little party crasher. Lucky he didn't make it onto the menu." Pulling out her knife she asks, "Would you like me to skewer him for you?" She is seemingly unconcerned about any threat.

%t"I wouldn't say no to that. Not for a moment!" Nara replies, shuddering as she looks down at the blazebug doing its mating dance to her camdroid. "But please be careful. I've no idea how dangerous it might be, and I don't want you getting hurt just to help /me/." She takes another slow step back, lowering her arm so Atsvara can get past, watching the insect warily...

%tThe blaze bug is seemingly unconcerned by the impending danger, advancing towards Nara and trying its best to interest a wrist mic in chitinous copulation while in its aphrodisiac induced state. The Xa Fel waiter remains frozen in place, unsure of how dangerous the insect truly is. His shorter leg twitches as if deciding whether or not to retreat the body its attached to.

%tAs Dooku's Droogs finish a set full of bizarrely esoteric Clone Wars themed songs with grating vocals by Count Binface, the rage metal band takes a break and the music in the room does a complete turn around with the Theelin Diva group on the white stage performing hauntingly beautiful songs that represent an elegy to the decline of their species.

%tAtsvara scoffs a little about the concern for her safety, "If I were in any real danger my security detail would be here. But there is cousin Janus drink at the bar. And brother Vee-Won...." She motions first to the bar where Janus sits before looking around the room spotting V1C3 looking at some of the busts of bioterrorists that line the walls, "....is admiring the art." Before Atsvara can skewer the bug in question May and her vornskr Myst arrive on the scene with the vornskr looking curiously at the bug."

%tNara takes another step back, accidentally drawing the wrist mount for her hand-held mic further away from the blazebug in the process. There's no telling how the bug will take this unexpected rejection! The blonde holojournalist barely glances at the two, offering a quick wave to cousin and brother respectively. Most of her attention is on the insect.%r%tAn exhalation from the vornskr is the only thing that draws her attention away from the amorous arthropod. The shadow-shrouded animal draws a little gasp from her. Poor May barely registers to her at all. First a /big/ bug, and now a ghost-beast!

%tThe blaze bugs amorous advances will not be deterred. Seeing its potential mate retreat, it glows brightly and opens its carapace. Wings beat to life, attempting to lift itself off and propel itself towards Nara's wrist mic. The flight is slow and clumsy, still affected by the chemicals in its system.
%tThe Xa Fel waiter lets out a gasp and begins to hobble away, trying his best to steady the Kullgroon Drops in his hands.

%tSoon this whole bug affair gathers more attention with Zubindi finally making his way toward the scene, "What is this? I did not order this entertainment." He motions Veruca over, "Did you hire this diminuitive model?" The Pantoran shakes his her head. Zubindi then crouches on the ground, "Is that you Malif? Did some evil witch turn you into a bug? Is that why you are not at the party?" From behind Zubindi, May protests, "I am not evil thank you ever much."

%tNara squeals again as the bug flaps up at her, backpedaling two hasty steps and ready to back up even more if necessary! The only thing that causes her to pause is Zubindi's arrival, and she watches him worriedly, just in case the insect unveils some even more horrible trick.%r%tShe waves to get Veruca's attention. "Better back up a bit, Miss. I've no idea if that bug's dangerous," she says softly, by way of warning.

%tThe blaze bug's drunken path suddenly takes a sudden turn as it momentarily loses control and collides into the cleavage of a nearby partygoer. She shrieks, "Get it off me!", while flapping her hands in exasperation. The blaze bug glows brightly in confusion as it tries to regain its bearings.

%tThe saga of the blaze bug soon gathers even more attention as the shrieking occurs somewhere near to V1C3 who is admiring the bust Jenna Zan Arbor. "Can't a droid just enjoy some art in peace? the pinstripe suit wearing mechanical being explains. Going over to the distraught party goer and plucking the bug off of her, he sterny looks the bug over, "You are under arrest for sexual assault. Anything you say can and may cause you to be squashed by the jackboot of justice."

%tNara breathes a sigh of relief as the bug is caught at last by V1C3. "Thank you," she says softly, giving the droid a grateful look. "It's probably silly of me, but I just /can't/ deal with insects... not at all. Thank you, too," she adds to Atsvara, who may or may not have re-stuffed her face by now.

%tin the grasp of its mechanical jailer, the blaze bug tries to bit the metal hand with its proboscis, all the while glowing brighter and radiating a palpable heat from its body. Its final victim huffs and hurries herself to the nearest bar without saying a thank you to the droid savior. "I need a drink," is all she says when she departs.

%t"Well you will want to steer clear of any Kubaz bearing a black platter of food than Nara cause it will be insects. Probably just steer clear of the black platters all together they are gross," remarks Veruca. Atsvara, face full of food again protests in between bites, "The Kullgroon Drops are delicious!" Veruca shakes her head at your friend, "You know where those grow right?" The golden-skinned girl smiles knowingly.

%t"Poor woman," Nara muses, watching the stressed-out woman rush to the bar. "But for an errant wingbeat, I'd be in her high-heeled shoes." She manages a shaky smile for Veruca, nodding. "I shall. Thank you so much for the warning. It's good to see you both again. Sorry for not telling you so earlier, but I was rather distracted at the time," she adds to the girls.%r%tShe looks at V1C3 gratefully. "You may want to get rid of that bug. It looks like it's trying to self-destruct or something like. Perhaps you could release it outside, or something like," she suggests. "I'd help you, but I can't force myself to come near it. I'm truly sorry." Knowing Nara, the apology is genuine.

%tA slightly inebriated, handsome looking fellow stumbles over towards the commotion, intrigued by the bug in V1C3's grasp. "Huh, isn't that the damndest thing you ever saw." He smiles widely and slowly turns his head towards Nara, "Hey... you look familiar."

%t"Perhaps so... it fell out of a plant and generally made itself scary for a minute or two, until that gallant droid apprehended it," Nara observes, blushing faintly as the man takes an interest in her. "Um, it's possible you have, actually. I'm Nara Rhen, and I produce a weekly holonet log about fashion and other things. And these are my friends Veruca, Atsvara, and VeeOne-CeeThree. All far more important people than I, really."

%t"Thank you, Veruca. Supposed to or not, it's very sweet of you, and I appreciate it almost as much as I appreciate you two," Nara replies, blushing slightly more at Veruca's praise. "Actually, I've been wondering about the theme for the party. Does anyone know what it's supposed to be? Something about 'Grotesques' and another word I just can't remember?"

%tThe man squints his eyes as he unabashedly gives Nara a look over. "Hmmm... no... I could have sworn I saw you covering swoop races. Are you sure?" he asks before turning his attention over to Atsvara and then Veruca. His eyes briefly widen a little bit, giving her a similar appreciative once over. "Well, I seem to find myself among some perfect company. Beauty, celebrity and credits. You all here together?" he asks. While the human is doing his best to lay on the charm, it's clear that he's got a bit of the liquid courage backing him up tonight. "Oh... the name is Tray Garos, by the way."

%t"Odalisques," interjects Vanilla as her rounds throw the hall bring her towards where our heroes are currently chatting. Wearing angel wings, a wooden tiara, and very little else the beauty confidently adds, "A fancy word for harem girls more or less." Zubindi adds, "Malif talked about Basca's aesthetic as beauty and beast. The vocabulary lacked a certain flair. Certainly lacked for any unique vision. So I jizzed it up a little."

%t"Well, I've done that once or twice... and some other events, too. Fashion is influenced by a wide variety of things, including politics, recreation, sports, and even war," Nara says, still blushing slightly, not missing how the man looks her over. "And we are not here together, but we /are/ acquainted, and we share some common interests."%r%tVanilla's arrival, especially her near-total lack of clothing, draws a deeper blush from Nara, and so does Zufindi's explanation. "Oh, my... the term would seem appropriate, and the renaming a clear improvement. Are you cold, Miss? I could see if I could find you a blanket," she offers.

%t"Let's not be too hasty. It's comfortable in here," Tray interjects with a smile. "Besides, I'm a big fan of the beauty aspect of this soiree. So, Nara, are you here to do a story on the new Biscuit Baron..ess?" he asks. While he does his best to give his attention to Nara, he is clearly distracted by Vanilla's attire, or rather lack of.

%tVanilla smirks, "This is more that I am use to wearing. I mean look at these wings they are massive. Can't strut on stage on them too easy." Zubindi adds, "A lot of the models here, that is the odalisques, are exotic dancers. Vanilla here has had quite a career at that, modelling for the likes of Jackoff Bollocks and Dandy Warton. High Priced Escort." Veruca interjects herself correct, "Very High Priced Escort." Vanilla nods and agrees, "Very High Priced Escort." Zubindi shrugs his shoulders a bit as if not considering the particular distinction important before adding, "She recently bought the Pallandorf Astoria in Plaxton back on Caspar. Really getting that place back into shape.

%t"Actually, I'm here to cover the party. Not that I mind talking to the new head of Biscuit Baron, if she welcomes company and conversation," Nara replies, still blushing, if not quite as brightly as before. She doesn't miss how Tray's attention is wandering to Vanilla, but the news about the model surprises her a little.%r%t"Wow... you've been very busy, Vanilla," she says warmly to Vanilla, her blush deepening again in spite of her best efforts. "Would you like to talk about your business dealings? I'm Nara Rhen, and I'd be glad to give you an interview, if you like," she continues.

%tTray takes the opportunity to sip at his Chandrilan whiskey while listening to the conversation. "She's a pretty big deal," he offers helpfully. "I've seen her on the holos." Taking a half step towards Nara, and in hopes to ingratiate himself to the woman, he adds, "You'd get a lot of publicity."

%t"I ain't lacking friend but I appreciate the...." Vanilla pauses as she replies to Nara and Tray, as if she isn't actually sure what she is supposed to be appreciative of. Motioning to another one of the models in the room with a party tit out, "Now poor Amidala over there. Excellent actress, great dancer, totally could have made it in Naboo security back in the day. Stuck showing her cooch at the Klit Kat Bar on Ngor Nghal."%r%tShe waves over to other woman in the distance. One of those affectations that appears like false display of friendliness on both their parts. Then turning back to Nara she adds, "Though perhaps an interview wouldn't be so bad when we are back on Caspar. Let folks know the hotel is more than just a place you can find a room by the hour." Winking at Tray she adds, "I expect you sir would want one for more than an hour."

%t"I'm not /that/ big of a deal... I really don't want to give her false hopes," Nara protests softly, her blush beginning to fade at last as she takes half a step back. "I'd be more than happy to give you that interview, Vanilla. Back on Caspar works fine. We can make contact on holonet to arrange a good time and place. And perhaps I should interview Amidala as well before I leave. A publicity bump at the right time could be just what her career needs to help her out of the gutter."

%tA mist of whiskey nearly escapes Tray's lips, but he manages to swallow it down instead and manages a couple of coughs and a smile back at Vanilla. He gives everyone a bashful shrug and says, "Well, it seems that she can read a room." He pauses for a moment, wondering if he made a pun, and shakes it off. "I'm sure that'll be a highly watched interview," he says to Nara. "Just make sure she's not smuggling in any of those glowing bugs, right?"

%tAs the blaze bug is mentioned again, Zubindi interjects "I am dreadfully sorry about that bug. It was a clear and unexpected lapse in security. I am still not entirely convinced that it was not Malif Tal-Tahn after some interaction with a folklore demon. I mean this whole party was his idea and he just doesn't show up. Clearly if he is not that bug he should be."

%t"No one would blame you or your staff for /that/ debacle, Zubindi. For what it may be worth, it wasn't a disaster. It livened things up a little, even if the party really didn't need any help," Nara observes, stifling an embarrassed chuckle behind a ladylike hand. "I'm glad VeeOne-CeeThree was able to catch it, though. Anyone else might have been burned."%r%tShe smiles at Vanilla, trying not to look below her neck and break out in a vivid blush all over again, something Vanilla is likely well aware of. "I'm sure it will be a very memorable interview. I suspect my fans will love getting to know such a famous young lady and budding entrepreneur."

%t"So, did any of you all actually know that guy?" Tray asks gesturing towards the alter with his whiskey glass. "I mean, I know about him and all. But, it seems like a lot of effort to celebrate... or mock him."

%tZubindi nods, "I did have dealings with him. Ironically enough he was engaging my services to help with some party as I recall. He was publically celebrated on Caspar for curing the Sariphage pandemic. Got main drag in Plaxton renamed after him in everything. Things got very sideways when it came out that he was the one who developed the virus to begin." Zubindi chuckles a bit adding, "Nothing about his aesthetic struck be as so unique that Malif should need to choose it as a party theme. I mean I talked to some other people who knew him. But ultimately just had to full in a lot of gaps myself the whole beauty and beast schtick."

%t"I never knew him. I've heard a lot things like what Mr. Zubindi said, though. Basically that he cured a pandemic he created himself. Not exactly someone you bring home to meet your parents, was he?" Nara says, shivering. "It definitely makes for a bizarre theme for a party. Maybe that's why Mr. Malif didn't show up?"

%t"That's a hell of a business plan, isn't it," Tray asks rhetorically. "Create a pandemic and its cure." He pauses only briefly and remarks, "He must've made a killing."%r%t"So what kind of someone would you bring to meet you parents, Nara?" Tray asks with a sly smile.

%tReeemerging in the group with a white platter of candied serrith bacon in her hands, Atsvara remarks, "He actually showed no interest in curing the pandemic at all when my father and his friends arrived in the system trying to help at the time. He did eventually when sufficiently bribed to, but his mercenary behaviour was the first indication that he was indeed a villain no matter what he presented to the public. It was only a matter of time before his crimes were going to be exposed." The girl then stuffs her face with food. Priorities.

%t"Well... nobody, actually. My parents passed away several years ago," Nara replies gently to Tray. She shudders at Atsvara's assessment of the dead man at the center of this very shindig. "We may be very thankful that he was exposed, even if it's a shame it didn't happen sooner. So many deaths would never have happened."

%tTray finishes the last sip of whiskey in his glass and stares at the empty vessel. Mustering a bit of sincerity, he offers to Nara, "My condolences." Cracking a smile he attempts to lighten the mood, "But on the bright side we're all hear to enjoy this party. Unfortunately, though, the drinks have run out. You all look like wine people. I'll go get us a bottle." He doesn't wait for confirmation or protest and in a chipper tone informs everyone, "Be back in a minute. Don't you all go wandering off on me."
